title: Feature Folder Locality
one_liner: Keep related code, tests, docs and configuration together — per feature, not per layer.
problem: Layer-first layouts scatter a single feature across controllers/, services/, models/, dto/, tests/, docs/. To understand or change it, an LLM (or human) has to stitch together files from six folders and re-derive the relationships every time. Context windows fill with navigation noise; subtle couplings hide between layers.
solution: Organize the tree by feature. Each feature folder contains its handlers, domain logic, data access, tests, fixtures, README and feature-specific config. Cross-cutting concerns live in clearly-named shared folders, and shared code is the exception, not the default. An LLM asked to change feature X needs to read mostly the X folder.
